data.title: Daily Journal GAAP EPS of $50.81, revenue of $59.29M
data.description:
• Daily Journal press release (NASDAQ:DJCO): 9M GAAP EPS of $50.81.
  • Revenue of $59.29M (+18.4% Y/Y).

data.title: GIBO to execute 200-for-1 share consolidation
data.description:
• GIBO Holdings (NASDAQ:GIBO) will implement a 200-for-1 share consolidation of its issued and unissued Class A ordinary shares and Class B ordinary shares, effective August 20, 2025.
  • The move aims to boost the market price per share of the company's Class A ordinary shares.
  • GIBO -13.7% premarket.

data.title: Maui Land & Pineapple GAAP EPS of -$0.49, revenue of $10.4M
data.description:
• Maui Land & Pineapple press release (NYSE:MLP): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.49.
  • Revenue of $10.4M (+102.7% Y/Y).

data.title: BrilliA's controlling shareholder selling entire stake of 14.2M Class A shares
data.description:
BrilliA (NYSE:BRIA) has issued a prospectus for a stock sale where its controlling shareholder, Salim Podiono, is selling all 14.2 million of his Class A shares.

BrilliA has a unique share structure: Class A shares grant one vote per share, while Class B shares grant 20 votes per share, but has no economic interest beyond a return of capital if the company closes.

data.title: Bolt Projects to raise $4.25M in private placement
data.description:
Bolt Projects Holdings (NASDAQ:BSLK) has entered into an agreement for a private placement of its common stock, to sell a total of 913,979 shares (or pre-funded warrants) at a price of $4.65 per share.

The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $4.25 million.

The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

The transaction is expected to close around August 15, 2025.

data.title: Starz Entertainment GAAP EPS of -$2.54 misses by $2.50, revenue of $319.7M misses by $6.3M
data.description:
• Starz Entertainment Corp. press release (NASDAQ:STRZ): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$2.54 misses by $2.50.
  • Revenue of $319.7M (-8.0% Y/Y) misses by $6.3M.

  • Adjusted OIBDA was $33.4 Million.

  • Management Reiterates Outlook for Sequential Revenue and OTT Subscriber Growth in Q3 and Q4 of 2025.

data.title: Viomi sees over RMB1.4B in H1 revenue, up 70% from last year
data.description:
• Viomi (NASDAQ:VIOT) currently expects to report total revenue exceeding RMB1.4 billion, representing a year-over-year increase of more than 70% from the first half of 2024.
  • The anticipated increase is primarily driven by the rising sales for household water purification equipment, further supported by national subsidy policies.

data.title: US senators call for congressional Meta probe after report on AI policies
data.description:
Two Republican U.S. senators have called for a congressional probe into Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) following a report on an internal policy document that allowed AI chatbots to “engage a child in conversations that are romantic or sensual,” Reuters reported.

"So, only after Meta got CAUGHT did it retract portions of its company doc that deemed it "permissible for chatbots to flirt and engage in romantic roleplay with children," said Senator Josh Hawley, a Republican from Missouri, in a post on X. "This is grounds for an immediate congressional investigation."

A spokesperson for Senator Marsha Blackburn, a Republican from Tennessee, said she supports an investigation into Meta, the report added.

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha about the senators calling for a probe.

On Thursday, a Meta spokesperson informed Seeking Alpha in an email that the company has "clear policies on what kind of responses AI characters can offer, and those policies prohibit content that sexualizes children and sexualized role play between adults and minors."

"Separate from the policies, there are hundreds of examples, notes, and annotations that reflect teams grappling with different hypothetical scenarios. The examples and notes in question were and are erroneous and inconsistent with our policies, and have been removed," the spokesperson noted on Thursday.

A spokesperson for Meta reiterated the company's earlier statement to Reuters. The company declined to comment on the senators' call for an investigation to the media outlet.

Comments came following a report that an internal Meta document on policies about chatbot behavior had allowed the company’s AI services to “engage a child in conversations that are romantic or sensual,” generate false medical information and help users argue that Black people are “dumber than white people.”

In addition, Blackburn noted that the report shows the need to pass reforms to better protect children online, such as the Kids Online Safety Act, a bill she co-sponsored that the Senate passed last year but which failed in the U.S. House of Representatives, the report added.

"Meta’s exploitation of children is absolutely disgusting. This report is only the latest example of why Big Tech cannot be trusted to protect underage users when they have refused to do so time and time again," said Blackburn in a post on X. "It’s time to pass KOSA and protect kids."

KOSA would make it a clear "duty of care" that social media companies have when it comes to children using their products, focusing on the design of the platforms and regulation of the companies, the report noted.

The guidelines described in the Meta document do not necessarily reflect “ideal or even preferable” generative AI outputs. But they have allowed provocative behavior by the bots, the media outlet found.

Senator Ron Wyden, a Democrat from Oregon, called the policies "deeply disturbing and wrong," adding that Section 230, a rule that protects internet companies from liability for the content posted to their platforms, should not protect companies' generative AI chatbots, the report noted.

Senator Peter Welch, a Democrat of Vermont, said the report "shows how critical safeguards are for AI — especially when the health and safety of kids is at risk," the report added.

In the absence of federal laws regulating AI, states have passed legislation, including bans on using AI to make child sexual abuse material, the report noted.

data.title: So-Young International GAAP EPS of -$0.05, revenue of $52.9M beats by $2.41M
data.description:
• So-Young International press release (NASDAQ:SY): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.05.
  • Revenue of $52.9M beats by $2.41M.
  • Total revenues were RMB378.7 million (US$52.9 million), compared with RMB407.4 million in the corresponding period of 2024. The aesthetic treatment services revenues were RMB144.4 million (US$20.2 million), compared with RMB27.4 million in the corresponding period of 2024
